name: Six-Vegetable Curry
recipeIngredient: 1 large Sweet Potato, Peeled And Cut
2 medium Turnips, Peeled And Cut Into 3/4-Inch Cubes
6 Fresh Green Chiles, Such As Jalapeno, Seeded And De-ribbed
1 tbsp Molasses, Dissolved In 3 Tbsp Water
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the potato, sweet potato, turnips, zucchini, eggplant, and string beans in a large bowl of ice water.
2. Heat oil in a large enameled cast-iron casserole.
3. Add onions and cumin seeds; cook over moderately low heat, stirring until onions soften (7-8 minutes).
4. Stir in chickpea flour and fenugreek; cook, stirring until the mixture forms a paste and browns slightly (2-3 minutes more).
5. Pour in 2 cups warm water and bring to a boil.
6. Cook, stirring until gravy begins to thicken (about 1 minute).
7. Add 2 more cups of water; bring to a boil again.
8. Reduce heat to low; cook until gravy reduces and thickens slightly (10 minutes).
9. Stir in chiles, ginger, ground coriander, turmeric, brown sugar, and salt; cook for 5 minutes longer.
10. Drain vegetables and add them to the gravy; stir in tomatoes and bay leaves.
11. Cover and cook until vegetables are tender but not mushy (20-30 minutes).
12. Stir in tamarind mixture and fresh coriander; cook uncovered for 5 minutes.
13. Discard bay leaves; serve with cooling fresh pineapple if desired.
